explicit_text: I was born and raised in Frankfort, Kentucky, growing up on a hobby farm on the rural west side of Franklin County. Frankfort, as the state capital, has always been a place where I felt deeply connected to community and nature, especially the creeks and river that inspire my art. My early experiences here shaped my identity as an artist and activist.

explicit_text: After college, I was awarded a Fulbright English teaching assistantship in Costa Rica for ten months. I chose Costa Rica to learn from their environmental education and public policies, hoping to bring insights back to Kentucky. The experience was eye-opening and helped me understand both Costa Rica and the United States from new perspectives.

explicit_text: Adrienne Maree Brown's work has been profoundly validating for me, especially her ideas about community as ecology and joy. Her books and blogs helped me articulate concepts I'd felt my whole life but struggled to express, particularly around resilience and interconnectedness. Her influence is evident in how I approach my art and community building today.
